brandAsset.name: Brand Essence
brandAsset.description: The heart and soul of your brand

proofPoints: Eigen zorgmethodiek ASBM (verwant aan Triple-C): relatie gaat voor prestatie
Specialisatie in EMB en MVG — complexe zorgvragen waar anderen afhaken
Structurele toerusting en scholing van medewerkers
Dragend netwerk van kerken, vrijwilligers en achterban
Verhalenformat 'Richting geven aan ___' met echte medewerkers
frameworkData.discriminator: Strictly identity-bound Reformed Christian care grounded in the Three Forms of Unity with an eternal perspective - not faith as an add-on, but as the foundation
frameworkData.audienceInsight: Reformed Christian families seeking disability care face a painful dilemma: choosing between professional quality care and care that honors their deepest convictions about human value, eternity, and God's Word. They don't want to compromise either.
frameworkData.emotionalBenefit: Gezien en gewaardeerd worden als schepsel van God. Voor medewerkers: een veilige haven waar geloof en werk samenvallen — men heeft aan een half woord genoeg. Voor cliënten en families: geborgenheid en onvoorwaardelijke ondersteuning.
frameworkData.essenceNarrative: Adullam is the place where people with disabilities find their true home - not just physically, but spiritually and communally. It's where dignity meets daily care, where God's Word shapes every interaction, and where 'goed dat je er bent' (good that you're here) isn't just a greeting but a foundational truth.
frameworkData.functionalBenefit: Professionele, identiteitsgebonden zorg volgens een beproefde methodiek (ASBM); voor medewerkers: toerusting, scholing en heldere professionele kaders bij complex werk.
frameworkData.selfExpressiveBenefit: Affirms commitment to Reformed Christian values and demonstrates belief that faith-based care is essential, not optional, for human flourishing

proofPoints: Daily Bible reading and prayer integrated into all care routines
Custom Biblical teaching method (Door Woord en Geest) developed specifically for clients
Network of church-based support committees ensuring community accountability
40+ years of consistent Reformed Christian care delivery
Multiple facility types (residential, respite, work, education) all under one Biblical framework
frameworkData.differentiator: De onlosmakelijke combinatie van identiteit én vakmanschap. Concurrenten in de christelijke zorg claimen naastenliefde (category-generic); Adullam verbindt het Bijbelse fundament aan methodische scherpte en specialisatie in complexe zorgvragen.
frameworkData.emotionalValue: Werk met eeuwigheidswaarde vanuit een diepe roeping; psychologische veiligheid van een gedeeld geloofsfundament — een groot hart.
frameworkData.targetAudience: (Toekomstige) medewerkers uit de reformatorische kring: zorgprofessionals, zij-instromers (o.a. uit bouw, ICT, retail) en starters.
frameworkData.functionalValue: Scholing en toerusting, heldere methodische kaders (ASBM), vakmanschap in complexe zorg — een sterke rug.
frameworkData.promiseOneLiner: A normal life, rooted in God's Word, where everyone belongs
frameworkData.coreCustomerNeed: Care for loved ones with disabilities that doesn't force a choice between professional quality and Biblical foundation - care where faith identity is preserved and central
frameworkData.promiseStatement: Bij Adullam krijg je toerusting om je gekregen talenten in te zetten voor de naaste — richting geven aan wat telt.
frameworkData.onlynessStatement: Only Adullam can provide comprehensive disability care across living, learning, and working that is strictly grounded in Reformed Christian identity because it was founded on and remains uncompromisingly committed to Biblical principles and the Three Forms of Unity since 1981
measurableOutcomes: Clients participate in daily spiritual practices appropriate to their level
Family satisfaction with faith-integration in care delivery
Client development in both practical skills and Biblical knowledge
Continuity of care without compromising Reformed identity
frameworkData.selfExpressiveValue: Demonstrates unwavering commitment to Reformed faith, affirms that Biblical worldview matters in every life aspect, shows love through identity-honoring care choices

brandAsset.name: Mission & Vision
brandAsset.description: What you do today and where you're headed

frameworkData.forWhom: Mensen met een verstandelijke of meervoudige beperking — in het bijzonder EMB en MVG — en hun families, primair binnen de reformatorische gezindte.
frameworkData.whatWeDo: Zorg, wonen en dagbesteding (leren, wonen en werken).
frameworkData.howWeDoIt: Identiteitsgebonden, vanuit Gods Woord en de Drie Formulieren van Enigheid, met het ASBM als methodisch kompas: onvoorwaardelijke begeleidingsrelatie, betekenisvolle dagbesteding, relatie gaat voor prestatie.
frameworkData.impactGoal: Het gewone leven, ook waar dat door complexe beperkingen niet vanzelfsprekend is.
frameworkData.boldAspiration: To be recognized as the standard-bearer for identity-bound disability care in the Netherlands, demonstrating that uncompromising Biblical foundation produces the highest quality of holistic human care
frameworkData.missionOneLiner: Enabling people with disabilities to live fully, grounded in God's Word
frameworkData.valuesAlignment: Mission and vision both flow from core conviction that every person has equal value before God (Reformed theology), that Biblical worldview must shape all of life including care delivery, and that 'normal life' for people with disabilities means full integration of faith and daily living
frameworkData.visionStatement: A Netherlands where every person with disabilities who seeks it can access care that fully honors their Reformed Christian identity, where 'normal life' means living with dignity before God, and where the disability care sector recognizes that Biblical worldview isn't optional but foundational to human flourishing
frameworkData.missionStatement: Cliënten 'het gewone leven' laten ervaren, aangepast aan hun mogelijkheden en talenten.
successIndicators: Duurzaam werkgeversmerk dat breed toepasbaar is (geen eenmalige campagne)
frameworkData.desiredFutureState: A thriving network of Adullam facilities where waiting lists are eliminated, where every Reformed Christian family has access to identity-bound care, where the model influences broader disability care sector, and where clients demonstrably flourish in all dimensions - physical, social, vocational, and spiritual
frameworkData.stakeholderBenefit: Families receive peace of mind and spiritual continuity; clients experience dignity and belonging; Reformed church community maintains care for vulnerable members; donors see tangible kingdom impact; society witnesses alternative care model grounded in human dignity
frameworkData.missionVisionTension: Mission focuses on current delivery of identity-bound care to those we serve today; Vision pushes toward sector influence and eliminating access barriers - requiring balance between maintaining strict Reformed identity (which limits scale) and expanding reach (which tests identity boundaries)

frameworkData.coreFear: That vulnerable individuals will be neglected, lose their identity, or be separated from their faith community
frameworkData.strategy: Create comprehensive care environments (living, learning, working) that integrate Reformed Christian worldview into every aspect of daily life
frameworkData.brandGoal: To provide identity-bound Christian care that enables people with disabilities to live a normal life rooted in Biblical values
frameworkData.coreDesire: De naaste beschermen, dienen en laten floreren — vanuit de opdracht van naastenliefde.
frameworkData.giftTalent: Deep commitment to identity-bound care that honors both individual dignity and Reformed Christian principles
frameworkData.subArchetype: Sage (de methodische wijsheid van ASBM: rust en kaders brengen in de chaos van moeilijk verstaanbaar gedrag)
frameworkData.shadowWeakness: May be perceived as exclusive or limiting to those outside the Reformed Christian community; potential for overprotection that limits independence
frameworkData.contentStrategy: Stories of individual growth and community belonging, testimonials from families, Biblical reflections on care and dignity, updates on care locations and activities
frameworkData.archetypeInAction: ASBM als begeleidingsfilosofie (relatie voor prestatie); welkomstpakket met kaart 'Goed dat je er bent'; onboarding als 100-dagenreis; waarderingsritme voor medewerkers; de medewerker als rustbrenger en 'filter' in de prikkelchaos van de cliënt.
frameworkData.customerExperience: Clients experience comprehensive care that addresses physical, emotional, educational, and spiritual needs within a consistent Biblical framework. Families experience relief knowing their loved ones are in identity-aligned care
frameworkData.marketingExpression: Warm, reassuring messaging that emphasizes safety, belonging, and spiritual grounding. Uses phrases like 'goed dat je er bent' to communicate unconditional acceptance
frameworkData.competitiveLandscape: Adullam differentiates through strict adherence to Reformed Christian identity (Drie Formulieren van Enigheid), offering families who prioritize this worldview a rare option for identity-bound care. Partners with Reformed church communities (Hersteld Hervormde Kerk, Gereformeerde Gemeenten) rather than competing with secular disability care providers
frameworkData.storytellingApproach: Narrative centers on God's love for all people, the inherent worth of every individual, and how Adullam serves as an extension of the church community's care responsibility
frameworkData.brandVoiceDescription: Warm én eerbiedig ('warm yet reverent'): pastorale zorg gecombineerd met praktische, professionele toerusting. Nuchter, gedragen, dienend — nooit speels, hip of overdreven energiek.

channelTones.email: Personal and pastoral, addressing families and supporters as members of a shared faith community
channelTones.crisis: Calm, transparent, and grounded in faith, emphasizing God's sovereignty while taking responsible action
channelTones.website: Informative and reassuring, balancing practical information about services with clear communication of Biblical foundation and Reformed identity
channelTones.socialMedia: Community-focused and celebratory, sharing stories of daily life, activities, and expressing gratitude for volunteers and donors
channelTones.customerSupport: Compassionate and patient, with deep understanding of family concerns and commitment to identity-bound care principles

frameworkData.writingSample: Bij Adullam geloven we dat ieder mens geschapen is naar Gods beeld en daarom van onschatbare waarde is. Of je nu een beperking hebt of niet, voor God zijn we allemaal gelijk. Daarom bieden we zorg en begeleiding die uitgaat van Gods Woord en de Drie Formulieren van Enigheid. In onze woonlocaties, werkplaatsen en leerprogramma's ervaar je een gewoon leven, aangepast aan jouw mogelijkheden en talenten. Goed dat je er bent.
frameworkData.colorDirection: Traditional and trustworthy palette likely featuring blues (trust, stability), greens (growth, care), and warm earth tones (community, home). Avoids overly bright or trendy colors in favor of timeless, reassuring hues
frameworkData.imageryDirection: Authentic photography showing real clients and staff in daily activities, emphasizing dignity, joy, and community. Images of home-like environments, group activities, and moments of connection. Biblical imagery and references to Reformed tradition may be incorporated subtly
frameworkData.typographyDirection: Clear, readable, and accessible typography that balances traditional authority with warmth. Likely uses serif fonts for headers (tradition, reliability) and sans-serif for body text (clarity, accessibility)
frameworkData.brandVoiceDescription: Adullam's voice is warm yet reverent, combining pastoral care with practical guidance. It speaks with the authority of Biblical conviction while maintaining accessibility and compassion. The tone is reassuring and community-focused, emphasizing shared values and collective responsibility for vulnerable members of the Reformed Christian community.

brandAsset.name: Brand Story
brandAsset.description: Your brand's past, present and future

frameworkData.theOutcome: Medewerkers die richting ervaren in hun vak, hun loopbaan en hun leven — en cliënten die daardoor het gewone leven ervaren.
brandThemes: Imago Dei - inherent worth and dignity
frameworkData.originStory: De naam Adullam verwijst naar de Bijbelse schuilplaats (de spelonk van Adullam, 1 Samuël 22) — een plek van toevlucht en bescherming. Die betekenis klinkt door in het Adullam Schutse Begeleidings Model.
proofPoints: Founded 1981, over 40 years of faithful service
Comprehensive services: living, learning, working, respite care
Custom Biblical teaching method (Door Woord en Geest)
Strong partnerships with Reformed church communities
Active volunteer and donor network (Vrienden van Adullam)
frameworkData.theSolution: Een werkgemeenschap met een gedeeld Bijbels fundament (psychologische veiligheid, 'aan een half woord genoeg'), het ASBM als professioneel kader tegen handelingsverlegenheid, en structurele toerusting om gekregen talenten te ontwikkelen.
frameworkData.abtStatement: Every person is created in God's image and deserves care that honors their whole being, BUT finding disability care that respects Reformed Christian identity is increasingly difficult in a secular society, THEREFORE Adullam provides comprehensive identity-bound care where people with disabilities can live, learn, and work within a Biblical framework, experiencing a normal life tailored to their talents while remaining rooted in their faith community.
frameworkData.theChallenge: Zorgprofessionals raken uitgeput in een sector met stijgende agressie (57% ervaart agressie) en hoog verzuim (7-8%). Zij-instromers ontvluchten de kille, targetgestuurde commerciële wereld op zoek naar existentiële betekenis. Reformatorische professionals missen in een seculariserende maatschappij een werkomgeving waar hun geloofsovertuiging vanzelfsprekend is.
frameworkData.worldContext: In a society where disability care increasingly operates from secular frameworks, families within the Reformed Christian community face a difficult choice: compromise their deeply held beliefs or struggle to find adequate care for their loved ones.
frameworkData.elevatorPitch: Bij Adullam werken we vanuit een diepe overtuiging: dat elke cliënt een mens van onschatbare waarde is, die de allerbeste zorg verdient. Dat klinkt misschien vanzelfsprekend, maar het bepaalt alles. Hoe we werken, hoe we naar elkaar kijken, en wat we verwachten van onszelf. Want hoe meer jij groeit als mens en professional, hoe meer jouw cliënt daarvan profiteert. Daarom investeren we in jou. In je vakmanschap, je persoonlijke groei en je welzijn. Niet als HR-beleid, maar als overtuiging. Zodat jij je talenten maximaal in kunt zetten voor de ander.
frameworkData.manifestoText:
We believe every person is created in God's image—of immeasurable worth, regardless of ability. In a world that increasingly separates care from faith, we stand firm: true care honors the whole person, body and soul, in light of eternity.

Adullam is more than care—it's community. It's the Reformed Christian family saying to our most vulnerable members: goed dat je er bent. You belong here. Your life has purpose. Your talents matter.

We provide what families in our community desperately need: a place where their loved ones can experience a gewone leven—a normal life—without compromising the Biblical worldview that shapes their identity. Where daily life is grounded in Gods Woord. Where learning, living, and working happen within the safety of shared faith.

This is our calling. This is our commitment. To care for the vulnerable as Christ calls us to, with excellence, faithfulness, and an eternal perspective. Because before God, we are all equal. And everyone deserves care that honors that truth.
valuesInAction: Daily Bible reading and prayer integrated into care routines
Tailored work opportunities in thrift stores and workshops that develop individual talents
Respite care (logeren) that provides relief for families while maintaining continuity of faith-based care
brandMilestones: 1981 - Adullam founded to provide Reformed Christian disability care
Development of Door Woord en Geest teaching method
Expansion to multiple care locations nationwide
Establishment of Vrienden van Adullam support network
Partnership with Reformed church communities (Hersteld Hervormde Kerk, Gereformeerde Gemeenten)
frameworkData.empathyStatement: We understand the weight of responsibility you carry and the deep desire to ensure your loved one receives care that honors both their needs and your shared faith. We know how difficult it is to find care that truly understands and respects your Reformed Christian identity.
frameworkData.founderMotivation: The conviction that every person, regardless of ability, is created in God's image and deserves care that respects their Reformed Christian identity and eternal perspective
frameworkData.coreBeliefStatement: We believe that every person is created in God's image and is of immeasurable value. Whether you have a disability or not, we are all equal before God. Therefore, care must be rooted in Biblical truth and honor the whole person—body, mind, and spirit.
frameworkData.authorityCredentials: Over 40 years of experience providing identity-bound care rooted in the Drie Formulieren van Enigheid, multiple care locations nationwide, partnerships with Reformed church communities, custom Biblical teaching method (Door Woord en Geest), and a comprehensive approach covering living, learning, and working
keyNarrativeMessages: Goed dat je er bent - unconditional acceptance and belonging
Identity-bound care that honors the whole person
A normal life tailored to individual talents and needs
Rooted in Biblical truth, focused on eternal perspective
Community care as a shared responsibility
frameworkData.philosophicalProblem: The belief that care should be separated from faith, treating people as merely physical beings rather than eternal souls created in God's image
frameworkData.stakesCostOfInaction: Without identity-bound care, individuals with disabilities may lose connection to their faith community, families experience ongoing stress and guilt, and the Reformed Christian community fails in its Biblical responsibility to care for the vulnerable among them
frameworkData.customerSuccessVision: Families experience peace knowing their loved one is thriving in an environment that honors their faith identity. Clients live with dignity, purpose, and joy, developing their talents and contributing to community life. The Reformed Christian community fulfills its calling to care for the vulnerable with both excellence and faithfulness.
frameworkData.transformationPromise: Medewerkers die richting ervaren in hun vak, hun loopbaan en hun leven — en cliënten die daardoor het gewone leven ervaren.
frameworkData.customerExternalProblem: Zorgprofessionals raken uitgeput in een sector met stijgende agressie (57% ervaart agressie) en hoog verzuim (7-8%). Zij-instromers ontvluchten de kille, targetgestuurde commerciële wereld op zoek naar existentiële betekenis. Reformatorische professionals missen in een seculariserende maatschappij een werkomgeving waar hun geloofsovertuiging vanzelfsprekend is.
frameworkData.customerInternalProblem: Fear that their loved one will lose connection to their faith identity, feel isolated from their church community, or be cared for by people who don't understand or respect their Biblical worldview

goals.title: Expand Identity-Bound Care Access
goals.description: Increase the availability of Reformed Christian disability care throughout the Netherlands so that every family who seeks identity-bound care can access it within reasonable proximity.
goals.theoryOfChange: By expanding geographic coverage and service capacity, more families will be able to access care that aligns with their faith identity, reducing the difficult choice between adequate care and faith community connection. This strengthens both individual wellbeing and community cohesion.
goals.measurableCommitment: Establish care locations in additional regions where Reformed Christian communities exist but currently lack access to identity-bound disability care

goals.title: Develop Talent-Based Work Opportunities
goals.description: Create more meaningful work and contribution opportunities that allow clients to develop their God-given talents and participate actively in economic and community life.
goals.theoryOfChange: When people with disabilities have opportunities to contribute through meaningful work, they experience greater dignity, purpose, and social connection. This challenges societal assumptions about disability and demonstrates the value every person brings to community life.
goals.measurableCommitment: Expand sheltered work programs and develop new partnerships that create diverse work opportunities tailored to individual abilities and interests

goals.title: Strengthen Community Care Network
goals.description: Deepen the involvement of the Reformed Christian community in supporting Adullam's mission through volunteerism, financial support, and active partnership.
goals.theoryOfChange: By mobilizing the broader Reformed Christian community to actively participate in care through volunteering and support, Adullam reinforces the Biblical principle of collective responsibility for vulnerable members. This creates a sustainable model of care rooted in community rather than solely institutional structures.
goals.measurableCommitment: Grow the Vrienden van Adullam network and increase volunteer engagement across all care locations, ensuring sustainable community-based support

frameworkData.mtpNarrative: Adullam envisions a future where no family in the Reformed Christian community must choose between quality care and faith identity for their loved ones with disabilities. We are working toward a comprehensive network of care that enables every individual to live, learn, and work in environments that honor both their needs and their eternal identity as image-bearers of God.

brandIntegration.positioningLink: These goals reinforce Adullam's positioning as the guide that enables the Reformed Christian community to fulfill its calling to care for vulnerable members with both excellence and faithfulness. The focus on expansion, talent development, and community engagement demonstrates commitment to comprehensive, sustainable care.
campaignDirections: Stories of individual talent development and meaningful work contributions
Testimonials from families about the peace and relief of identity-aligned care
Calls to action for community involvement through Vrienden van Adullam
brandIntegration.internalActivation: Staff and volunteers are regularly reminded that their work contributes to a larger vision of comprehensive Reformed Christian care. Training emphasizes both professional excellence and spiritual grounding. Success stories of client development and community impact are celebrated as evidence of progress toward transformative goals.
communicationThemes: Every person has God-given talents worth developing
Identity-bound care as a right, not a privilege
Living with purpose and eternal perspective

frameworkData.massiveTransformativePurpose: To ensure every person with disabilities in the Reformed Christian community experiences a life of dignity, purpose, and belonging rooted in Biblical truth.

brandAsset.name: Core Values
brandAsset.description: Fundamental beliefs that guide your brand
brandAsset.content: Officiële kernwaardencirkel Adullam — hart van de cirkel: "De Bijbel leert God lief te hebben boven alles en onze naaste als onszelf." De vijf waarden daaromheen: (1) Afhankelijkheid — we zijn in alles afhankelijk van God; we leven in eeuwigheidsperspectief en hebben geborgenheid in Christus nodig. (2) Veiligheid — we werken in een reformatorische omgeving waarin we menswaardigheid en veiligheid bieden. (3) Verbinding — we hebben andere mensen nodig om te groeien, om mens te zijn; we bieden onvoorwaardelijke ondersteuning door te zorgen voor respect, aandacht en vertrouwen; we zijn er voor elkaar: altijd en overal. (4) Verantwoordelijkheid — we dragen verantwoordelijkheid en zijn bereid verantwoording af te leggen; we hebben binnen eigen draagkracht genoeg ruimte om keuzes te maken. (5) Dienstbaarheid — we ontwikkelen onze gekregen talenten en zijn daarmee van betekenis; we zijn het elkaar verschuldigd perspectief te bieden. Gebruiksregel: verwijs in teksten naar deze kernwaarden, maar benoem ze niet steeds expliciet — de beleving moet voelbaar zijn (show, don't tell).

ownValue.name: Afhankelijkheid
ownValue.description: Het meest onderscheidende — in alles afhankelijk van God, leven in eeuwigheidsperspectief, geborgenheid in Christus. Geen enkele seculiere werkgever kan dit bieden.

anchorValue1.name: Verbinding
anchorValue1.description: Vandaag al waargemaakt in de onvoorwaardelijke ondersteuning (respect, aandacht, vertrouwen) en de dragende gemeenschap — 'we zijn er voor elkaar: altijd en overal'.

anchorValue2.name: Veiligheid
anchorValue2.description: De reformatorische omgeving waarin menswaardigheid en veiligheid geboden worden — voor cliënten én medewerkers ('aan een half woord genoeg').

frameworkData.valueTension: Groot hart ↔ sterke rug: verbinding en veiligheid zonder verantwoordelijkheid en dienstbaarheid worden soft; professionele scherpte zonder verbinding wordt kil. Afhankelijkheid houdt beide geworteld: vakmanschap is instrument van naastenliefde, nooit doel op zich.

aspirationValue1.name: Dienstbaarheid
aspirationValue1.description: De beweging van het werkgeversmerk — gekregen talenten ontwikkelen en daarmee van betekenis zijn; elkaar perspectief bieden.

aspirationValue2.name: Verantwoordelijkheid
aspirationValue2.description: Verantwoordelijkheid dragen én verantwoording afleggen; professionele ruimte binnen eigen draagkracht (de 'sterke rug').

brandAsset.name: Social Relevancy
brandAsset.description: Your brand's societal and environmental impact
brandAsset.content: Governance: identiteitsgebonden governance op grondslag van de Bijbel en de Drie Formulieren van Enigheid. (Verder aanvullen vanuit jaarverslag/governancecode.)

frameworkData.impactNarrative: While Adullam's primary focus is providing identity-bound care rather than broad social activism, the organization's work inherently addresses social issues around disability inclusion, dignity, and the right to faith-aligned care. By creating comprehensive living, learning, and working opportunities, Adullam demonstrates that people with disabilities can contribute meaningfully to society when given appropriate support.
frameworkData.impactStatement: Zorg, wonen en dagbesteding voor een kwetsbare doelgroep (EMB/MVG); werkgelegenheid met structurele toerusting; hechte verbinding met kerken, vrijwilligers en de achterban; het mogelijk maken van 'het gewone leven' voor mensen voor wie dat niet vanzelfsprekend is.
keyStakeholders: Reformed Christian families with disabled members
Local communities where care locations operate
activationChannels: Educational programs about disability and faith
communicationPrinciples: Emphasize inherent dignity and worth of every person
Share stories of individual growth and community contribution
Advocate within Reformed Christian community for responsibility toward vulnerable members
Demonstrate through action rather than public campaigns

brandAsset.name: Purpose Statement
brandAsset.description: The reason your organization exists beyond profit

frameworkData.mechanism: Identiteitsgebonden zorg en begeleiding volgens het ASBM (Adullam Schutse Begeleidings Model): relatie gaat voor prestatie, onvoorwaardelijke ondersteuning, gedragen door een geloofsgemeenschap van medewerkers, kerken en vrijwilligers.
frameworkData.statement: Vanuit naastenliefde ieder mens — geschapen naar Gods beeld en van onschatbare waarde — het gewone leven laten ervaren.
frameworkData.pressureTest: Als deze purpose waar is: investeren in medewerkers is geen HR-beleid maar overtuiging (toerusting); moeilijk gedrag wordt niet bestraft maar begrepen als uiting van stress of onmacht; beslissingen worden getoetst aan het Bijbelse fundament en niet louter aan bedrijfsmatige logica.
frameworkData.impactDescription: Cliënten met een (ernstig meervoudige) beperking of moeilijk verstaanbaar gedrag ervaren het gewone leven, aangepast aan hun mogelijkheden en talenten. Medewerkers ontwikkelen hun gekregen talenten om er beter te kunnen zijn voor de naaste (rentmeesterschap).

brandAsset.name: Golden Circle
brandAsset.description: Simon Sinek's WHY → HOW → WHAT framework

how.details: De warme, familiale cultuur (het grote hart) wordt gecombineerd met methodische scherpte, professionele distantie en standvastigheid bij complexe zorgvragen (de sterke rug). Vakmanschap is niet het doel maar het instrument om naastenliefde professioneel en verantwoord in praktijk te brengen.
how.statement: Identiteitsgebonden zorg vanuit een professioneel-methodisch kader (ASBM) én een dragende geloofsgemeenschap: groot hart, sterke rug.

why.details: Naastenliefde is bij Adullam geen abstract begrip maar een actieve opdracht — de motor achter alles, zonder er pocherig over te doen.
why.statement: God liefhebben boven alles en onze naaste als onszelf. Elke cliënt is een uniek schepsel van onschatbare waarde en verdient onze onverdeelde aandacht en de allerbeste zorg.

what.details: Specialisatie in complexe zorgvragen: EMB (ernstig meervoudig beperkten) en MVG (moeilijk verstaanbaar gedrag).
what.statement: Landelijk zorg, wonen en dagbesteding bieden aan mensen met een verstandelijke of meervoudige beperking, vanuit de reformatorische identiteit.

brandVoiceguide.voiceDescription: De stem van Adullam is warm én eerbiedig: pastorale zorg gecombineerd met praktische toerusting. Nuchter, ingetogen, gedragen, oprecht, betrouwbaar en vakkundig. Adullam spreekt met de rust van Bijbelse overtuiging en blijft daarbij toegankelijk en compassievol. In elke tekst klinken het grote hart (zorgzaamheid, gemeenschap) en de sterke rug (vakmanschap, methodische scherpte) samen. Vuistregel: schrijf zoals je praat tegen iemand die je vertrouwt — rustig, gemeend, zonder overdrijving of verkooptaal.
contentGuidelines: Kernparadox in balans: elke tekst laat zowel het grote hart (zorgzaamheid) als de sterke rug (vakmanschap) zien — alleen hart wordt te zacht, alleen rug wordt kil.

Medewerkersverhalen volgen het vaste format: kop 'Richting geven aan ___', drieluik vak (ASBM-casus) / loopbaan (toerusting, rentmeesterschap) / leven (het gewone leven). Lang: 600-800 woorden + fotoreportage + video 90-120 sec. Kort: één richtingmoment, 15-30 sec.
Groei altijd koppelen aan de ander (rentmeesterschap), nooit aan zelfontplooiing.

Kernwaarden beleven, niet benoemen: de vijf kernwaarden (afhankelijkheid, veiligheid, verbinding, verantwoordelijkheid, dienstbaarheid) klinken door in de beleving van de tekst, maar worden niet steeds expliciet genoemd of opgesomd — dan ligt het er te dik bovenop. Show, don't tell.
De Bijbelse identiteit is invoelbaar aanwezig, maar nooit pretentieus of 'te zwaar'.
Referral en waardering framen als gunnen, niet als verdienen.
AVG bij verhalen: expliciete toestemming met gebruikstermijn, vertrekscenario vastgelegd, inzage vóór publicatie, cliënten alleen herkenbaar met toestemming van de wettelijk vertegenwoordiger.
Crisiscommunicatie: rustig, transparant, gegrond in geloof (Gods soevereiniteit), met concrete verantwoordelijke actie.
Zorgcontact: compassievol en geduldig, met oog voor identiteitsgebonden zorgprincipes.
writingGuidelines: Geen uitroeptekens, hypewoorden of jeugdig jargon.
Rustige, gemeende zinnen zonder overdrijving of verkooptaal; geen superlatieven.
Vermijd zware, stichtelijke marketingtaal — respectvol en nuchter.
Toon is nooit 'bold', 'playful', 'innovative', 'modern' of 'energetic' — die generieke marketingdimensies passen niet bij Adullam.
Controleer elke tekst vóór publicatie met de 10-punts controlelijst uit de Schrijfwijzer Adullam v1.0.
vocabularyDo: Goed dat je er bent (corporate baseline)
vocabularyDont: training / persoonlijke ontwikkeling (als kaal HR-begrip — gebruik 'toerusting')
brandVoiceguide.voiceSample: Bij Adullam geloven we dat ieder mens geschapen is naar Gods beeld en daarom van onschatbare waarde is. Of je nu een beperking hebt of niet, voor God zijn we allemaal gelijk. Daarom bieden we zorg en begeleiding die uitgaat van Gods Woord en de Drie Formulieren van Enigheid. In onze woonlocaties, werkplaatsen en leerprogramma's ervaar je een gewoon leven, aangepast aan jouw mogelijkheden en talenten. Goed dat je er bent.
writingSamples: Bij Adullam werken we vanuit een diepe overtuiging: dat elke cliënt een mens van onschatbare waarde is, die de allerbeste zorg verdient. Dat klinkt misschien vanzelfsprekend, maar het bepaalt alles. Hoe we werken, hoe we naar elkaar kijken, en wat we verwachten van onszelf. Want hoe meer jij groeit als mens en professional, hoe meer jouw cliënt daarvan profiteert. Jouw ontwikkeling ís naastenliefde in de praktijk. Daarom investeren we in jou. In je vakmanschap, je persoonlijke groei en je welzijn. Niet als HR-beleid, maar als overtuiging. Zodat jij je talenten maximaal in kunt zetten voor de ander.

ads.description: Werving/vacatures: nuchter, concreet vakmanschap voorop; 'Richting geven aan …' als terugkerende kapstok, per doelgroep ingevuld.

email.description: Persoonlijk en pastoraal; families en betrokkenen aanspreken als leden van dezelfde geloofsgemeenschap.

video.description: Het verhalenformat: lang 90-120 sec, kort richtingmoment 15-30 sec — echte medewerkers, geen acteurs of stockgevoel.

website.description: Informatief en geruststellend; praktische informatie in balans met een heldere, ingetogen Bijbelse identiteit.

socialMedia.description: Gemeenschapsgericht en dankbaar; verhalen uit het gewone leven, waardering voor collega's, vrijwilligers en donateurs — nooit hip of speels om zichzelf.

antiPatterns: "Hoe rijk wil je worden?" (afgewezen concept — associatie materialisme)
"Naastenliefde in de praktijk" als centrale claim (afgewezen — te zacht, category-generic)
Bonus/beloning als communicatieboodschap (frame is gunnen, niet verdienen)

examplePhrases.text: Bij Adullam krijg je toerusting om je gekregen talenten te ontwikkelen — voor de ander.
Waar geef jij richting aan — in je vak, je loopbaan, je leven?
Wij zijn een gedreven en nuchtere zorgorganisatie, met een groot hart en een sterke rug.
Onze cliënten zijn mensen die, met onvoorwaardelijke ondersteuning, het gewone leven ervaren.
Adullam. Goed dat je er bent.
Bij Adullam kun je de beste versie van jezelf worden.
Wij zijn een energieke, speelse en moderne zorgorganisatie.
Onze cliënten zijn klanten die zelfstandig kunnen wonen.

logoGuidelines: Primary logo available in RGB format (ADULLAM-logo-RGB.jpg) and SVG vector format
Maintain clear space around the logo equal to the height of the 'A' in ADULLAM
Use the SVG version for digital applications to ensure crisp rendering at all sizes
Logo should be displayed on light backgrounds (warm peach #FFF8F5 or white)
Minimum size: ensure logo text remains legible, recommend minimum width of 120px for digital
The logo incorporates the brand's forest green (#24823C) as a primary color element
logoDonts: Don't place the logo on busy backgrounds that reduce legibility
Don't stretch, distort, or alter the proportions of the logo
Don't use the JPG version when SVG is available for digital applications
Don't recreate or retype the logo text using different fonts
Don't apply drop shadows, gradients, or effects to the logo
Don't place the logo on dark backgrounds without using an inverted/white version
colorDonts: Don't use pure black (#000000) for large text blocks—use the primary Forest Green (#24823C) or Slate Gray (#8A8A8A) instead
Don't combine the orange colors with reds or pinks—maintain clear color separation
Don't use the Mint Whisper (#EEFBF4) accent background for critical information—it's too subtle
Don't mix warm (orange/peach) and cool (blue) accent colors in the same component
Don't use colors outside the defined palette—this dilutes brand consistency
Don't place Forest Green text on the Mint Whisper background—insufficient contrast

photographyStyle.mood: RECOMMENDED: Warm, authentic, and hopeful. Images should feel candid and genuine rather than staged, capturing real moments of connection, dignity, and everyday life. Natural lighting preferred to convey openness and warmth.
photographyStyle.subjects: RECOMMENDED: People with disabilities engaged in ordinary activities (working, learning, socializing), staff-client interactions showing genuine relationships, community integration moments, hands at work, faces showing concentration or joy, group activities that demonstrate belonging
photographyStyle.composition: RECOMMENDED: Eye-level or slightly lower angles to convey equality and respect. Medium shots and close-ups that capture emotional connection. Include environmental context that shows 'normal life' settings (workshops, homes, community spaces). Avoid institutional-looking backgrounds.

photographyGuidelines: RECOMMENDED: Prioritize dignity and agency—show clients as active participants, not passive recipients of care
RECOMMENDED: Capture authentic moments of work, learning, and daily life rather than posed 'care scenarios'
RECOMMENDED: Include diversity in age, ability levels, and activities to reflect the full spectrum of the community
RECOMMENDED: Show staff-client relationships that feel collaborative and respectful, not hierarchical
RECOMMENDED: Incorporate subtle visual references to faith (e.g., people in prayer, Bible study groups) without making it the dominant visual theme
RECOMMENDED: Use natural, unfiltered photography that avoids overly polished or corporate aesthetics
RECOMMENDED: Include images of the broader community (volunteers, families, local businesses) to emphasize integration and connection
illustrationGuidelines: RECOMMENDED: Use simple, warm line illustrations for icons and wayfinding that feel hand-drawn rather than corporate
RECOMMENDED: Develop illustration style that represents people with disabilities with dignity—avoid infantilizing or overly simplified representations
RECOMMENDED: Consider illustrative elements that reference the ASBM values circle mentioned in content, using warm colors and organic shapes
RECOMMENDED: Use illustrations sparingly to support photography, not replace it—real people and real stories should dominate visual communication
imageryDonts: Avoid clinical or institutional imagery (hospital beds, medical equipment as focal points, sterile environments)
Don't show people with disabilities only as recipients of help—balance with images of contribution and capability
Avoid overly sentimental or pitying visual narratives that diminish dignity
Don't use stock photography—authenticity is critical for this brand's credibility
Avoid imagery that separates people with disabilities from 'normal life'—no segregated or institutional-only settings
Don't make religious imagery heavy-handed or exclusive—it should feel integrated, not imposed
Avoid corporate or overly polished aesthetics that create distance between the organization and the people it serves

graphicElements.usageNotes: The design system emphasizes clean, sharp geometric shapes with minimal decorative flourishes. The primary visual interest comes from content organization and interactive elements rather than ornamental graphics. The sharp 0px border-radius on buttons and containers creates a modern, structured aesthetic, while subtle 2px rounding on thumbnails softens media elements.
visualDevices: Icon and text pairings for navigation and features
Swiper carousel components for content presentation
decorativeElements: Subtle thumbnail borders with 2px radius for media elements
Clean white space as primary decorative element

graphicElementsDonts: Avoid heavily rounded corners on primary UI elements (buttons, cards, containers) - maintain the sharp 0px aesthetic
Don't introduce ornate decorative patterns that conflict with the minimal, content-first approach
Avoid heavy borders or outlines - the system uses 0px borders for clean separation
Don't mix rounded and sharp corner styles inconsistently - reserve subtle rounding (2px) only for media thumbnails

patterns: RECOMMENDED: Subtle dot or line grid patterns for background differentiation if needed
textures: Smooth, flat surfaces with no visible texture
RECOMMENDED: Very subtle noise texture (2-3% opacity) for depth on large background areas
patternsTextures.usageNotes: The design follows a minimal, texture-free approach that prioritizes content readability and clean visual hierarchy. Backgrounds are primarily solid colors with no visible patterns or textures, creating a professional, accessible interface suitable for the care and support services context.
backgrounds: Predominantly white or light neutral backgrounds
Solid color sections for content separation
RECOMMENDED: Light gray (#F5F5F5 - #FAFAFA) for alternate sections

iconographyStyle.colorUsage: Primary brand color for active states and emphasis, neutral gray tones for inactive or secondary icons, maintaining accessibility and clear visual hierarchy
iconographyStyle.usageNotes: Icons utilize FontAwesome and Swiper icon libraries, following a line-based style with consistent stroke weight. The 2px corner radius on icons matches the subtle rounding used for thumbnails, creating visual cohesion. Icons serve functional purposes in navigation, feature highlights, and interactive elements like carousels.

iconographyDonts: Don't mix filled and outlined icon styles within the same interface section
Avoid inconsistent stroke weights - maintain 1.5px across all line icons
Don't use icons smaller than 16px for accessibility reasons
Avoid decorative icons that don't serve a functional purpose - keep icon usage purposeful and clear

gradientsEffects.name: RECOMMENDED: Primary Brand Gradient
gradientsEffects.usage: Hero sections, call-to-action overlays, feature highlights

gradientsEffects.name: RECOMMENDED: Subtle Overlay Gradient
gradientsEffects.usage: Image overlays to ensure text readability on hero images

gradientsEffects.name: RECOMMENDED: Accent Gradient
gradientsEffects.usage: Secondary CTAs, hover states, interactive element highlights

layoutPrinciples.gridSystem: 12-column responsive grid with fluid gutters, max-width container for content sections
layoutPrinciples.usageNotes: The layout system prioritizes clarity, accessibility, and structured content presentation appropriate for a care organization. The sharp geometric approach with 0px borders creates professional, trustworthy layouts, while the 4px-based spacing scale ensures consistent rhythm throughout the interface. The system accommodates interactive elements like flipbooks and carousels while maintaining clean, scannable content hierarchies.
compositionRules: Content sections use consistent 48-64px vertical spacing between major blocks
Cards and containers use 24-32px internal padding for comfortable content presentation
Interactive elements (buttons, links) maintain minimum 8-12px spacing for touch accessibility
Sharp 0px border-radius on primary containers creates clean, structured layouts
Subtle 2px border-radius reserved exclusively for media thumbnails and image elements
FlipBook and carousel components use the swiper library for consistent interactive patterns
Typography and content maintain consistent left/right padding of 16-24px on mobile, 32-48px on desktop
layoutPrinciples.whitespacePhilosophy: Generous whitespace creates breathing room and emphasizes the care-focused, accessible nature of the organization. Clean spacing supports readability and reduces cognitive load for users seeking support services.

styleguideColor.name: Forest Green
styleguideColor.notes: Primary brand color defined as --color-primary and --color-green-dark. Used for main brand elements and primary actions.

styleguideColor.name: Vibrant Orange
styleguideColor.notes: Secondary brand color defined as --color-orange. Provides energetic contrast to the primary green.

styleguideColor.name: Fresh Green
styleguideColor.notes: Lighter green accent defined as --color-green-light. Used for positive actions and highlights.

styleguideColor.name: Deep Green
styleguideColor.notes: Dark green hover state defined as --color-green-hover. Used for interactive element states.

styleguideColor.name: Mint Whisper
styleguideColor.notes: Very light green accent background defined as --color-accent-green. Used for subtle highlighting.

styleguideColor.name: Burnt Orange
styleguideColor.notes: Darker orange variant defined as --color-orange-dark. Used for hover states and emphasis.

styleguideColor.name: Ocean Blue
styleguideColor.notes: High-frequency blue (91 occurrences). Used extensively for links, buttons, and interactive elements.

styleguideColor.name: Slate Gray
styleguideColor.notes: Dark gray defined as --color-grey-dark. Used for secondary text and muted content.

styleguideColor.name: Warm Peach
styleguideColor.notes: Warm off-white background color defined as --color-body-bg. Creates a soft, welcoming base for content.

styleguideColor.name: Coral Blush
styleguideColor.notes: Soft coral background defined as --color-friends-of-bg. Used for special sections or callouts.

brandRule.message: Avoid "klant / consument (voor cliënten)" — listed in Brand Voiceguide.wordsWeAvoid.
Avoid "inclusief (in brede, seculiere zin)" — listed in Brand Voiceguide.wordsWeAvoid.
Avoid "diversiteit (als los containerbegrip)" — listed in Brand Voiceguide.wordsWeAvoid.

brandRule.pattern: "hoe rijk wil je worden?" (afgewezen concept — associatie materialisme)
brandRule.message: Anti-pattern — never use ""hoe rijk wil je worden?" (afgewezen concept — associatie materialisme)" (Brand Voiceguide).

brandRule.pattern: "naastenliefde in de praktijk" als centrale claim (afgewezen — te zacht, category-generic)
brandRule.message: Anti-pattern — never use ""naastenliefde in de praktijk" als centrale claim (afgewezen — te zacht, category-generic)" (Brand Voiceguide).
Anti-pattern — never use ""richting geven aan verlangen" (expliciet afgewezen)" (Brand Voiceguide).
Anti-pattern — never use ""grote opdrachtgever" / "heilig werk" (te zwaar, stichtelijk, pretentieus)" (Brand Voiceguide).
Anti-pattern — never use "hypewoorden, uitroeptekens, jeugdig jargon" (Brand Voiceguide).

brandRule.pattern: bonus/beloning als communicatieboodschap (frame is gunnen, niet verdienen)
brandRule.message: Anti-pattern — never use "bonus/beloning als communicatieboodschap (frame is gunnen, niet verdienen)" (Brand Voiceguide).

persona.name: De zij-instromer
persona.tagline: Ik wil werk dat er echt toe doet — voor langer dan dit leven.
persona.occupation: Nu werkzaam in bouw, ICT of retail; oriënteert zich op de zorg
goals: Werk met existentiële betekenis (eeuwigheidswaarde)
Een carrièreswitch die verantwoord is voor het gezin
frustrations: De kille, targetgestuurde commerciële wereld
Onzekerheid: kan ik dit wel — zorg zonder zorgachtergrond?
persona.strategicImplications: Benadruk toerusting en het ASBM-kader: je hoeft het niet alleen te kunnen, je wordt toegerust. Route loopt altijd via een kennismakingsgesprek (belangstelling ≠ geschiktheid).
persona.quote: In mijn huidige werk draait alles om targets. Ik wil iets doen wat betekenis heeft.

persona.name: De zorgprofessional-switcher
persona.tagline: Ik ben het vak niet moe — wel de omstandigheden.
coreValues: Geloofsidentiteit ook op het werk kunnen leven
goals: Werken met heldere methodische kaders bij complexe zorgvragen
Een team dat als gemeenschap draagt
frustrations: Agressie zonder professionele kaders (57% van zorgmedewerkers ervaart agressie)
Hoog verzuim en uitputting in de sector (7-8%)
Geloofsovertuiging steeds moeten uitleggen in een seculiere werkomgeving
persona.strategicImplications: Leid met de sterke rug: ASBM, specialisatie EMB/MVG, toerusting. De identiteitslaag ('aan een half woord genoeg') is de doorslaggevende emotionele bonus.
persona.quote: Ik wil uitgedaagd worden op mijn vakmanschap, niet weggezet worden als oppas.

persona.name: De starter
persona.tagline: Mijn eerste baan moet passen bij wie ik ben.
goals: Een goede start met begeleiding en ontwikkelruimte
frustrations: Angst om in het diepe gegooid te worden bij complexe zorg
persona.strategicImplications: Onboarding als 100-dagenreis en het welkomstpakket ('Goed dat je er bent') waarmaken wat de campagne belooft; korte verhalen (richtingmomenten, 15-30 sec) passen bij dit kanaalgebruik.

persona.name: Marieke van der Berg, The Devoted Reformed Mother
persona.location: Barneveld or rural Netherlands (Bible Belt region)
persona.occupation: Homemaker or part-time work in education/care sector
persona.personalityType: Marieke is deeply rooted in her Reformed Christian faith and prioritizes her family's spiritual wellbeing above all else. She is caring, community-oriented, and seeks environments that align with her Biblical worldview. She values tradition, stability, and the teachings of the Three Forms of Unity.
coreValues: Dignity and equality before God for all people
interests: Supporting charitable causes within her faith community
goals: Find trustworthy care for her adult child with disabilities that honors their Reformed faith
Ensure her child experiences spiritual growth and Biblical teaching
Maintain connection with a community that shares her values
Secure respite care that provides continuity of Christian guidance
motivations: Providing the best possible care within a Biblical framework
Ensuring her child's eternal perspective is nurtured
Finding relief and support from like-minded caregivers
Maintaining her child's dignity and sense of belonging in God's creation
frustrations: Secular care facilities that don't align with Reformed Christian values
Lack of understanding of identity-bound care needs
Difficulty finding respite care that maintains spiritual continuity
Concern about her child's spiritual development in mainstream settings
behaviors: Seeks recommendations from church community and pastor
Prioritizes organizations with explicit Reformed Christian foundations
Actively involved in church committees and support groups
Researches care providers' theological positions and teaching methods
Values long-term relationships with care providers
preferredChannels: Referrals from pastors and church leaders
persona.quote: I want my child to grow in faith and experience God's Word daily, not just receive care. Adullam understands that our identity in Christ comes first.
persona.bio: Marieke is a devoted mother of three from the Dutch Bible Belt, whose youngest child has an intellectual disability. She is deeply involved in her local Gereformeerde Gemeente and seeks care that honors her family's Reformed Christian identity. For Marieke, finding Adullam meant finding a place where her child could live a normal life while growing spiritually.
buyingTriggers: Explicit commitment to Biblical Reformed worldview
Daily Bible reading and prayer as part of care
Alignment with Three Forms of Unity
Testimonials from other Reformed Christian families
Need for respite care or transition to adult care
decisionCriteria: Theological alignment with Reformed Christian doctrine
Quality of spiritual guidance and Biblical teaching
Safety and professionalism of care environment
Proximity to home and church community

persona.name: New Persona
persona.tagline: Caring with conviction, growing in faith
interests: Professional development in the care sector
Family and home life in Barneveld
goals: Find a workplace grounded in biblical principles
Grow professionally within the care sector
Work in a collaborative and respectful team environment
Achieve a sustainable work-life balance in a demanding sector
motivations: Living out biblical values of naastenliefde in daily work
Being part of a team that genuinely supports one another
Making a meaningful difference in the lives of patients
Working for an employer who respects and invests in staff
frustrations: Employers who place excessive demands without adequate support
High physical and emotional workload in healthcare
Workplaces that do not live by stated values
Lack of development opportunities in care organisations
behaviors: Seeks employers through faith-based and community networks
Evaluates workplaces based on cultural and values fit
Invests in professional development when given the opportunity
Prioritises team cohesion and collaborative working styles
persona.quote: I want to work somewhere that truly lives by its values — where we care for each other just as much as we care for our patients, and where faith is not just a word but the foundation of everything we do.
buyingTriggers: Clear demonstration of biblical and ethical values by the brand
Evidence of a supportive and collaborative team culture
Visible investment in employee development and wellbeing
Authentic testimonials from like-minded care professionals
decisionCriteria: Alignment of organisational values with biblical principles
Quality of team culture and collegial support
Availability of professional growth and training opportunities
Proximity to Barneveld or flexible working options

persona.name: Henk Visser, The Committed Church Volunteer
persona.occupation: Retired tradesman or small business owner
persona.personalityType: Henk is a pillar of his local Reformed community, known for his practical support and commitment to Christian charity. He believes strongly in the Biblical mandate to care for the vulnerable and sees supporting Adullam as a calling. He is hands-on, community-focused, and values tangible contributions.
coreValues: Biblical mandate to care for the vulnerable
interests: Thrift store shopping and supporting social enterprises
goals: Support Adullam financially and practically as a 'Vriend van Adullam'
Contribute to maintaining identity-bound care in the Netherlands
Volunteer time and skills to help the organization
Encourage others in his community to support Adullam
Ensure continuity of Reformed Christian care for future generations
motivations: Living out his faith through practical service
Making a tangible difference in vulnerable people's lives
Fulfilling Biblical calling to care for those with disabilities
Building community and fellowship through shared mission
frustrations: Secularization of care institutions in the Netherlands
Difficulty maintaining identity-bound care due to regulations
Lack of awareness about Adullam's mission in broader community
Financial challenges facing Christian care organizations
behaviors: Regular donor to Reformed Christian causes
Recruits other donors and volunteers from his network
Attends Adullam information sessions and events
persona.quote: Supporting Adullam is not just charity—it's our calling to ensure our brothers and sisters with disabilities can live and grow in God's Word.
persona.bio: Henk is a retired carpenter and active member of his local Hersteld Hervormde Kerk. He serves on multiple church committees and has been a 'Vriend van Adullam' for over a decade. He regularly volunteers at Adullam's thrift stores and encourages his business network to support the organization's mission of identity-bound care.
buyingTriggers: Appeals highlighting need for identity-bound care
Stories of impact on clients' spiritual and daily lives
Challenges to Reformed Christian care infrastructure
Personal connection to families using Adullam services
decisionCriteria: Organization's commitment to Biblical Reformed principles
Opportunities for personal involvement beyond financial giving
Alignment with Three Forms of Unity

persona.name: Johannes de Vries, The Young Adult with Intellectual Disability
persona.location: Netherlands, living at Adullam residential facility
persona.occupation: Participant in sheltered workshop or day activities
persona.education: Special education, ongoing learning through Adullam programs
persona.income: Disability benefits and supported work income
persona.personalityType: Johannes is a gentle, faith-oriented young man who values routine, community, and spiritual guidance. He thrives in structured environments where he feels safe and valued. He enjoys contributing through work and finds meaning in his daily Bible readings and prayer times.
coreValues: Daily spiritual guidance and Bible teaching
Being valued as equal before God
interests: Work activities at sheltered workshop or thrift store
goals: Live a normal life tailored to his abilities
Grow in understanding of God's Word
Contribute meaningfully through work activities
Maintain friendships within his community
Experience safety and belonging
motivations: Desire to be valued and included
Spiritual growth and understanding Biblical teachings
Comfort of routine and familiar caregivers
Fellowship with others who share his faith
frustrations: Feeling different or excluded from mainstream society
Difficulty with complex tasks or rapid changes
Limited independence in daily life
Challenges in expressing needs and preferences
behaviors: Participates in daily Bible reading and prayer sessions
Attends work activities at sheltered workshop regularly
Engages in community living with support staff
persona.quote: Goed dat je er bent—I feel at home here, and I learn about God every day. My work at the thrift store makes me proud.
persona.bio: Johannes is a 28-year-old resident at one of Adullam's living facilities. He has an intellectual disability and has been part of the Adullam community for five years. He works three days a week at an Adullam thrift store and participates in the 'Door Woord en Geest' learning program, where he continues to grow in his understanding of Scripture at his own pace.

product.name: Wonen (Residential Care)
product.description: Residential care facilities providing a safe, home-like living environment for people with disabilities, featuring 24/7 professional care and daily Biblical guidance. Locations nationwide offer community living rooted in Reformed Christian values, where clients experience a normal life tailored to their needs and talents.
features: Daily Bible reading and prayer sessions
Individual care plans tailored to needs and talents
Professional staff trained in identity-bound care
benefits: Safe and secure living environment
Identity-bound Christian care aligned with Reformed faith
Community support and fellowship
Spiritual growth through daily Biblical teaching
Normal life experience adapted to individual abilities
Continuity of faith-based values from family home
Equality and dignity before God for all residents
useCases: Long-term residential care for adults with intellectual disabilities
Transition from family home to independent living with support
Community living for individuals seeking Reformed Christian environment
Permanent housing for people with disabilities requiring daily guidance

product.name: Werken (Day Activities & Sheltered Work)
product.description: Meaningful day activities and sheltered work opportunities tailored to individual abilities and talents, including operation of thrift stores (Kringloopwinkels). Professional guidance helps clients develop skills, contribute to society, and experience purpose through work within a Reformed Christian context.
features: Sheltered workshops adapted to individual capabilities
Tailored activities matching talents and abilities
Biblical worldview integrated into daily work
benefits: Sense of purpose and meaningful contribution
Skill and talent development at appropriate pace
Social interaction and friendship building
Pride in work accomplishments
Integration of faith and daily activities
Structured routine and stability
Community involvement through thrift store operations
useCases: Day activities for adults with disabilities living at home or in residential care
Sheltered employment for individuals unable to work in mainstream settings
Skill development programs for people with intellectual disabilities
Social enterprise participation through thrift store work

product.name: Leren (Learning & Development Programs)
product.description: Educational programs and developmental guidance using the custom 'Door Woord en Geest' (Through Word and Spirit) Biblical teaching method. Programs are tailored to age and cognitive level, enabling individuals with disabilities to grow spiritually and personally at their own pace within a Reformed Christian framework.
features: Custom Biblical teaching method (Door Woord en Geest)
Tailored to individual age and cognitive level
Appropriate learning pace for each individual
Integration with daily care and activities
benefits: Personal development at individual pace
Spiritual growth and Biblical understanding
Age-appropriate learning materials
Cognitive development suited to abilities
Eternal perspective and faith formation
Dignity through learning and growth
Connection to Reformed Christian heritage
useCases: Biblical education for children and adults with intellectual disabilities
Ongoing spiritual development programs for residential clients
Faith formation tailored to cognitive abilities
Supplementary education aligned with Reformed Christian worldview

product.name: Logeren (Respite Care)
product.description: Short-term stay and respite care facilities providing temporary relief for parents and primary caregivers while maintaining continuity of Reformed Christian care. Dedicated locations like Beekheim and Kroonheim offer professional guidance in a safe, fun environment that honors the family's faith identity.
features: Continuity of Christian care and values
benefits: Relief and rest for primary caregivers and parents
Fun and safe environment for clients
Continuity of Reformed Christian care
Peace of mind for families
Temporary break without compromising faith values
Professional support during family emergencies or vacations
Maintained spiritual routines and Bible teaching
useCases: Planned respite breaks for family caregivers
Trial stays before transitioning to residential care

product.name: Wonen & zorg
product.description: Woonlocaties waar cliënten met een verstandelijke of meervoudige beperking het gewone leven ervaren, met zorg en begeleiding volgens het ASBM en vanuit de reformatorische identiteit. Specialisatie in complexe zorgvragen (EMB, MVG).
features: Huiselijke, veilige omgeving binnen de geloofsgemeenschap

product.name: Dagbesteding (leren en werken)
product.description: Betekenisvolle dagbesteding in werkplaatsen en leerprogramma's, aangepast aan de mogelijkheden en talenten van de cliënt — leren, wonen en werken als samenhangend geheel.
features: Betekenisvolle activiteiten die gekregen talenten ontwikkelen
Onderdeel van de ASBM-driehoek: begeleidingsrelatie, dagbesteding, het gewone leven

competitor.name: Sirjon
competitor.description: Christelijke/identiteitsgebonden gehandicaptenzorg met vergelijkbare doelgroep en achterban. Strategische notitie: Adullams onderscheid ten opzichte van identiteitsgenoten is de koppeling van identiteit aan methodisch vakmanschap (ASBM) en specialisatie in complexe zorgvragen (EMB/MVG).
competitor.toneOfVoice: Gebruikt vergelijkbare naastenliefde-claims — reden waarom 'Naastenliefde in de praktijk' voor Adullam onvoldoende onderscheidend was (category-generic in de christelijke zorg).
